acceptance_targets: [chapter_04_award_001] Pass if the revised ending shows another named staff witness confirm the shortage on page, or else the ending no longer claims confirmed scale and closes on a different concrete turn that matches what Carmen has actually verified by the final paragraph. Fail if the chapter still exits on future-tense anticipation of a count that has not happened yet.

[chapter_04_craft_001] Pass if every Room 110 reference in chapter_04 names the same resident identity used elsewhere in the allowed context, or if none of those references name the resident at all. Fail if a cold read of adjacent chapter material still produces two different women occupying Room 110.

[chapter_04_prose_001] Pass if a reader can parse the revised line once and the wage math reads plausibly without having to reinterpret the word differential. Fail if the sentence still implies that 3.50 dollars times sixteen hours covers 140 dollars plus gas.

[chapter_04_prose_002] Pass if the revised passage conveys Carmen's legal exposure through one specific concrete consequence or action and then moves directly into the next tactical beat. Fail if the passage still stacks abstract authorities and policy language to restate stakes the previous count scene has already made clear.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised ending shows another named staff witness confirm the shortage on page, or else the ending no longer claims confirmed scale and closes on a different concrete turn that matches what Carmen has actually verified by the final paragraph. Fail if the chapter still exits on future-tense anticipation of a count that has not happened yet.
findings.problem: The chapter's second promised proof never becomes on-page fact. After Carmen's solo recount, the ending keeps saying she needs the next formal count as witness, so the 47-pill scale remains anticipated rather than incontrovertibly landed. That leaves the chapter reading like setup for chapter_05 instead of a fully delivered discovery unit, even though the allowed context for chapter_04 says Carmen should end this chapter with proof of both Harry's capability and the diversion scale.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:125, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:131 so the chapter either stages the witnessed shift-change count on page before the ending or explicitly recalibrates the ending to a smaller state change and stops presenting the 47-pill shortage as already confirmed fact.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if every Room 110 reference in chapter_04 names the same resident identity used elsewhere in the allowed context, or if none of those references name the resident at all. Fail if a cold read of adjacent chapter material still produces two different women occupying Room 110.
findings.problem: Chapter 04 identifies Room 110's resident as Mrs. Okafor, but the provided boundary pack's immediate next-chapter excerpt identifies Room 110 as Mrs. Delacroix. Because Room 110 is used as a persistent auditory landmark across adjacent chapters, this reads as a canon-level resident identity contradiction rather than a harmless naming variation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:103, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:127 so Room 110 aligns with the same resident identity used in the allowed context packs, or remove the resident's name entirely and keep only the recurring sound cue if the room is meant to stay anonymous here.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can parse the revised line once and the wage math reads plausibly without having to reinterpret the word differential. Fail if the sentence still implies that 3.50 dollars times sixteen hours covers 140 dollars plus gas.
findings.problem: Carmen's POV is supposed to live in exact numbers, but this sentence makes the 3.50-dollar overnight differential sound like it alone closes a 140-dollar insurance gap across sixteen hours. Readers who do the arithmetic get yanked out of the scene, and the financial pressure that should feel precise instead reads fuzzy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:5 so the money logic is explicit on the page: either make it clear that the total pay for the extra sixteen hours closes the gap, not the differential alone, or change the shortfall or hour-count so the stated math visibly works.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage conveys Carmen's legal exposure through one specific concrete consequence or action and then moves directly into the next tactical beat. Fail if the passage still stacks abstract authorities and policy language to restate stakes the previous count scene has already made clear.
findings.problem: Once the count scene has already made Carmen's exposure legible, the narration shifts into abstract institutional paraphrase: Controlled Substances Act, Board of Nursing, prosecutor, policy, then another summary of why she needs proof. The chapter starts explaining its own stakes instead of letting the cabinet, the log, the towel, and the shift-change clock carry them.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or replace snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:123, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:125 with a more concrete immediate beat anchored to the log, the supervisor call she is not making, or Harry's tactical advantage, so the passage advances pressure instead of re-translating what the reader already understands.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
